data.title: Stock futures fall amid US-Iran tensions & disappointing news from Walmart. Dow Jones -0.12%, S&P 500 -0.08%, Nasdaq 100 -0.08% dip._trade deficit at $70.3 billion. Jobless claims drop to 206,000. FedWatch projects 94% likelihood of unchanged rates in March. DoorDash stock rises 13.57% premarket despite weak Q4 results.
data.description: U.S. stock futures dropped on Thursday following a rise the previous day, with all major benchmark indices seeing a decrease. The U.S. trade deficit widened to $70.3 billion in December due to lower exports and higher imports. Initial jobless claims fell to 206,000, lower than anticipated. The Federal Reserve meeting minutes showed disagreement among officials on interest rate futures, with talks of rate increases to combat inflation. Indirect Iran-U.S. nuclear talks stalled, pushing oil prices up. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.10%, with a 3.47% yield on the two-year bond. The FedWatch tool projected a 94% likelihood of rates remaining unchanged in March. Dow Jones, S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, and Russell 2000 indices were down. ETFs SPY and QQQ tracking S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 were also lower in premarket trading. DoorDash stock rose 13.57% despite weak Q4 results, with positive forecasts for Q1.

data.title: ESS appoints Randall Selesky as Chief Commercial Officer to boost global expansion efforts.
data.description: Wilsonville, Oregon - ESS has named Randall Selesky as their Chief Commercial Officer to drive worldwide expansion efforts. This strategic move aims to bolster growth for the company.

data.title: Golf Entertainment Group Inc. (OTC: GLFE) upgraded to OTCQX Best Market on Feb 19, 2026. Access financial info at www.otcmarkets.
data.description: Golf Entertainment Group Inc. (GLFE) has disclosed that its shares are now tradable on the OTCQX Best Market since February 19, 2026. The OTCQX platform, managed by OTC Markets Group, will provide investors access to GLFE's financial disclosures and stock quotes on www.otcmarkets.com. GLFE is a prominent entity in the golf leisure and entertainment sector.

data.title: VPN.com CEO urges Apple to acknowledge Christian names like Yeshua and Yahuah not recognized by its software.
data.description: In a recent press release dated February 19, 2026, it was announced that Michael Gargiulo, the CEO of VPN.com and a respected premium domain broker, has called for Apple to acknowledge important names in Christianity that are currently not recognized by its dictionary software. The names in question are Yeshua and Yahuah. Mr. Gargiulo has emphasized the significance of these names and their long-standing importance within the Christian community. He has urged Apple to rectify this issue to ensure inclusivity and accuracy in their software.

data.title: Leica Gallery LA presents Broad Strokes 4 exhibition celebrating Women's Month with photographers Mandy Walker, Ruby Bell, Katarina Benzova, and Eva.
data.description: In honor of International Women's Month, Leica Gallery Los Angeles will showcase Broad Strokes 4, a group exhibit highlighting the diversity and talent of women photographers. The event will feature works by prominent artists including Mandy Walker, Ruby Bell, Katarina Benzova, and Eva. The exhibition opens on Feb. 19, 2026, in Teaneck, N.J., celebrating the power and creativity of female photographers in capturing various aspects of life. This initiative represents a significant step towards recognizing and promoting women's contributions in the field of photography.

data.title: Valmet to boost wood handling capacity with new log line for The Price Companies in Georgia.
data.description: Valmet Oyj, a company based in Espoo, Finland, announced a new development on February 19, 2026, at 3:00 p.m. EET. They have confirmed receiving an order from The Price Companies to supply a new debarking and chipping line. The purpose of this line is to enhance the wood chip supply capacity at one of The Price Companies' sites in Georgia. This agreement signifies a significant investment in the operational capabilities of the Georgia facility.
MJLB prepares Better Pets for expansion amid rising global pet wellness market, set to reach $275 billion by 2025.

data.title: Black Hills Corp. requests rate review in South Dakota to recover costs for electric service to 75,000 customers.
data.description: In Rapid City, South Dakota, on February 19, 2026, Black Hills Corp. disclosed that its electric utility, listed as BKH on NYSE, has submitted a rate review application to the South Dakota Public Utilities Commission (PUC). The application is seeking approval for the recuperation of essential capital infrastructure and operational expenses crucial for maintaining the delivery of secure and dependable electric services to around 75,000 customers across South Dakota.

data.title: ASP Isotopes forms advisory board for nuclear fuel sector, appointing key leaders to provide expertise.
data.description: Prominent leaders in nuclear fuel and advanced materials have been named as the first members of the Scientific Advisory Board (SAB). The board includes experts with extensive experience in the field, with a combined total of over 50 years of research and leadership. This diverse SAB will provide crucial guidance and insights on important developments in the industry.

data.title: TPN.health hits 100k+ verified behavioral health providers, across all 50 states, advancing digital care infrastructure.
data.description: On February 19, 2026, TPN.health revealed that it has exceeded 100,000 confirmed behavioral health professionals listed on its platform nationwide. This achievement spans across all 50 states and serves as a significant milestone in showcasing a new and effective strategy in addressing the ongoing challenges within the behavioral health sector.

data.title: Advantum Health promotes Nancy Nelson to VP of Coding Operations. Nelson will oversee medical coding, compliance, and more.
data.description: In Louisville, Kentucky on February 19, 2026, Advantum Health shared that Nancy Nelson has been appointed as the Vice President of Coding Operations. As part of her new position, Nelson will oversee all medical coding operations, clinical documentation improvement, compliance, and more within the organization.

data.title: Carvana's Q4 revenue exceeds estimates at $5.60B, with lower earnings per share. CEO projects significant growth in 2026.
data.description: Carvana Co (CVNA) announced strong fourth-quarter sales, revealing $5.60 billion revenue, surpassing analyst predictions of $5.26 billion. Despite this success, their Q4 earnings of $4.22 per share did not align with estimates. Ernie Garcia, Carvana's CEO, highlighted a 43% year-over-year growth in 2025. The company achieved record unit economics and enhanced customer value with improved selection, quicker delivery times, and reduced costs. Carvana anticipates substantial growth in retail units sold and adjusted EBITDA in 2026, with projections indicating a sequential increase in both metrics. More on this development is available for reading on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Code Platoon updates curriculum to AI + Fullstack Engineering, adapting to tech workforce demands.
data.description: In Chicago on February 19, 2026, Code Platoon introduced its latest AI + Fullstack Engineering program, adapting to the shifting needs of the tech industry. This updated curriculum represents the organization's next phase, offering a more immersive experience for aspiring tech professionals.

data.title: Security Vision recognized as a leader in 2025 SPARK Matrix™ for Security Analytics and Automation by QKS Group, top ratings in technology excellence.
data.description: The QKS Group SPARK Matrix™ recently conducted a competitive analysis and ranking of top Security Analytics and Automation vendors. Security Vision emerged as a top contender, impressing with its advanced technology and multi-layered security architecture. The company received high ratings in various areas, showcasing its prowess in the industry.

data.title: MCA named top Nokia mission-critical partner in North America for 2025, excelling in communication solutions.
data.description: Mobile Communications America, Inc. (MCA), a company based in Spartanburg, South Carolina, was acknowledged for its excellence in critical infrastructure and innovation on February 19, 2026. The company is a prominent player in providing integrated communication, connectivity, and security solutions at a national level. MCA's recognition as the top performer by Nokia underscores its leadership in the industry. This achievement speaks to MCA's commitment to delivering top-notch services.

data.title: Supio launches AI features to reduce case prep costs, streamline operations, proactive legal insights. #AIinnovation
data.description: Supio, the top AI platform in the personal injury space, has introduced new features that streamline case synchronization, ledger generation, and exhibit preparation. These enhancements empower firms with accurate figures and enhance their negotiating power during settlement talks. The announcement was made on February 19, 2026, in Seattle.

data.title: AEVEX appoints Christi Morrison as Chief Legal Officer to oversee global legal functions, ensuring continued growth.
data.description: In Solana Beach, California, AEVEX has appointed Christi Morrison as the new Chief Legal Officer. With a background as a senior legal executive and advisor, Morrison will lead legal, compliance, and governance areas globally. Her role will involve supporting AEVEX's expansion and operational efficiency, providing guidance to the executive team and Board of Directors. Morrison's appointment signifies a strategic move for the company, aiming to strengthen its legal framework under her leadership.

data.title: Archer chooses Bristol as UK Engineering Hub for advanced initiatives; hundreds of applications received since December.
data.description: Archer (NYSE: ACHR) has chosen Bristol as the location for its UK Engineering Hub to facilitate cutting-edge engineering projects for both commercial and defense programs. Bristol's rich history in aerospace innovation and skilled workforce make it a prime spot for Archer's initiatives. Following the company's reveal of the UK hub plans in December, it has attracted hundreds of applications.
